what_it_does: Browser runtime bundle generated from dc-runtime TypeScript sources. It loads React UMD, parses <x-dc> design-component documents, compiles templates into React element builders, instantiates user logic classes, manages a registry with subscriptions, loads external JSX/TSX modules via Babel, handles streaming placeholders and head injection, and exposes a window API for editor integration.
observations: The file is a generated IIFE with a header comment telling users not to edit it directly.
It loads React and ReactDOM from unpkg with hardcoded integrity hashes.
It defines a custom template syntax using {{...}} interpolation, <sc-for>, <sc-if>, <dc-import>, and <x-import>.
Template HTML is encoded so camelCase attributes and raw table elements survive DOM parsing.
It compiles attributes into getter functions that resolve against a value map at render time.
It includes CSS for streaming placeholders with a shimmer animation and error overlays.
It uses new Function to evaluate user-provided logic scripts and external module code.
It has a per-component error boundary implemented in StreamableComponent.
It supports external JSX/TSX modules by loading Babel standalone and transpiling in the browser.
It exposes window APIs such as __dcUpdate, __dcSetProps, __dcBoot, __dcAnnotatedTemplate, and __dcTemplateSource.
It manages <sc-helmet> content by injecting or updating tags in document.head.
It detects circular component imports through an ancestor context chain.
standouts: Streaming-aware placeholder UI with CSS shimmer animation.
On-demand Babel transpilation for <x-import> JSX/TSX modules.
Annotated template output used by an external editor to map rendered nodes back to source.
Per-component error boundary that renders an inline error overlay and placeholder.
Registry versioning with subscriber notifications for hot updates.
conventions: Generated bundle that should be rebuilt rather than hand-edited.
Self-contained IIFE with no external module imports.
UMD script injection for React dependencies.
React class components for stateful logic and function wrappers for subscriptions.
camelCase attribute encoding via sc-camel-* prefixes before DOM parsing.
Lowercase event attributes mapped to React camelCase handler names.
Use of new Function as a sandbox for user logic and external scripts.
Set-based subscription pattern for registry change notifications.
concerns: User logic and external modules are executed via new Function, which is a security risk for untrusted input.
Scripts are injected from external CDNs with no fallback or CSP discussion in the file.
Direct innerHTML assignment is used with a nosemgrep suppression comment.
The generated file contains complex runtime logic but is marked non-editable, making debugging harder.
External module loading pollutes window globals and may conflict with host page code.
Hardcoded unpkg URLs and SRI hashes tie the runtime to specific React versions.
notes: This is the in-browser execution layer for a design-component format: it turns .dc.html templates into React trees and wires them to an editor host.
questions: What host editor consumes the __dcAnnotatedTemplate and __dcTemplateSource APIs?
How is streaming mode triggered, and what protocol pushes partial html/js updates?
What is the intended deployment environment for this runtime (local file, iframe, web app)?
How are sibling .dc.html components resolved relative to the boot URL?
What does the $preview metadata control beyond the FULL_PAGE_CSS injection?
Are there tests or example .dc.html documents that exercise these features?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header says it is generated from dc-runtime and is the runtime that boots and renders design components.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make HTML-first design components runnable inside any browser page with minimal host setup, while giving an editor enough hooks to push incremental updates and map rendered output back to source nodes.
novelty: A custom template language compiled to React in the browser, streaming shimmer placeholders, on-demand Babel-transpiled x-imports, and an annotated-template bridge for source-to-rendered node mapping.
contribution: Provides the end-to-end client-side rendering engine for the design-component system: parsing, compilation, React lifecycle integration, user logic execution, external module loading, streaming UI, head management, and the editor-facing update/inspection API.
summary_for_embedding: A generated browser runtime that parses <x-dc> templates, compiles them to React elements, runs user logic classes, loads external JSX modules, and exposes an editor bridge API.
intention_for_embedding: To render design-component (.dc.html) documents interactively in a browser with live updates, streaming previews, and editor integration.

symbols.does: Defines or assigns an object property with enumerable/configurable/writable descriptors.
symbols.description: Helper used by the bundler to emulate class field assignment, choosing Object.defineProperty when the key already exists so descriptors are preserved.

symbols.does: Assigns a public class field value, coercing non-symbol keys to strings.
symbols.description: Bundlers class-field helper that delegates to __defNormalProp; used to install fields like state and __host on class instances.

symbols.does: Returns the global window.React object or throws.
symbols.description: Accessor that verifies React is loaded before use; called frequently by builder functions to create elements.

symbols.does: Returns the global window.ReactDOM object or throws.
symbols.description: Accessor that verifies ReactDOM is loaded; used during boot to render the root component.

symbols.does: Shorthand for getReact().createElement.
symbols.description: Convenience wrapper used throughout the runtime to construct React elements without importing React directly.

symbols.does: Parses a live DOM document for an <x-dc> block and its script.
symbols.description: Extracts the template HTML, logic script source, props, and preview metadata from a document already loaded in the page.

symbols.does: Parses a raw HTML string for an <x-dc> block.
symbols.description: Uses regex to locate the <x-dc> region and DOMParser to extract the logic script and data-props, returning template/js/props/preview.

symbols.does: Parses the data-props JSON and separates $preview from user props.
symbols.description: Returns preview metadata and the remaining props object, ignoring keys that start with $.

symbols.does: Derives a component name from a URL pathname.
symbols.description: Decodes the last path segment and strips .dc.html or .html extensions, defaulting to Root.

symbols.does: Chooses the root component name based on the boot document location.
symbols.description: Prefers the current location pathname if it ends with a dc.html extension, otherwise falls back to the document base URI.

symbols.does: Decodes a URI string, returning the original on failure.
symbols.description: Small helper used when normalizing paths so malformed percent-encoding does not crash.

symbols.does: Mounts the runtime into the current document.
symbols.description: Parses the document, registers the root component, replaces <x-dc> with a #dc-root host, injects full-page CSS when not in preview mode, and renders a StandaloneRoot via ReactDOM.

symbols.does: React wrapper that re-renders when the root registry entry changes.
symbols.description: Subscribes to the registry entry and renders the root DC component with any prop overrides.

symbols.does: Registry subscription callback that increments local tick state.
symbols.description: Used inside StandaloneRoot to trigger a re-render when the runtime bumps the registry version.

symbols.does: Evaluates a simple template expression against a value map.
symbols.description: Supports parenthesized expressions, equality operators, boolean/string/number literals, negation, and path lookups including bracket indexing.

symbols.does: Checks whether outer parentheses wrap the entire expression.
symbols.description: Used by resolve to safely unwrap a parenthesized expression without splitting on internal parentheses.

symbols.does: Locates a top-level ===, !==, ==, or != operator.
symbols.description: Walks the expression respecting bracket/paren depth so nested comparisons are not mistaken for top-level operators.

symbols.does: Resolves a dotted/bracketed identifier path from a value map.
symbols.description: Starts with an identifier and then traverses .property or [key] segments, returning undefined for missing paths.

symbols.does: Encodes template HTML for DOM parsing and React compatibility.
symbols.description: Expands self-closing imports, renames helmet tags, converts camelCase attributes to sc-camel-* kebab form, and aliases raw table tags so the DOM parser preserves them.

symbols.does: Converts kebab-case strings to camelCase.
symbols.description: Used when decoding encoded attributes back into React prop names.

symbols.does: Converts a CSS declaration string into a JavaScript style object.
symbols.description: Splits on semicolons, maps kebab property names to camelCase except for CSS variables, and trims values.

symbols.does: Compiles an attribute value into a getter function.
symbols.description: Returns a function that either resolves a single {{...}} expression, concatenates mixed literal/expression parts, or returns the raw string.

symbols.does: Collects React props and pseudo-class hints from a DOM node.
symbols.description: Iterates attributes, decodes camelCase keys, maps class/for/event names, compiles values, and records style-* pseudo classes and hint-size.

symbols.does: Extracts host-level positioning styles from a style value.
symbols.description: Filters a style object to only the properties that affect component host layout, such as position, inset, width, height, z-index, and transform.

symbols.does: Compiles template HTML into a render function.
symbols.description: Encodes the HTML, stamps data-dc-tpl indices, walks the DOM tree to produce builder functions, and returns a render function that produces React elements.

symbols.does: Executes all child builders against values and context.
symbols.description: The function returned by compileTemplate; it maps each builder over the values/context and returns an array of React nodes.

symbols.does: Walks child nodes and returns their builder functions.
symbols.description: Helper used recursively by walk to compile each child into a builder, filtering out null builders.

symbols.does: Dispatches a DOM node to the appropriate compiler.
symbols.description: Routes text nodes, sc-for, sc-if, x-import, sc-helmet, dc-import, and plain elements to their specialized walkers.

symbols.does: Logs a warning once per template for unresolved holes.
symbols.description: Deduplicates warnings by context name and message so missing values do not flood the console outside streaming mode.

symbols.does: Compiles a text node containing {{...}} interpolations.
symbols.description: Splits text into literal and expression parts; unresolved expressions render as empty, editor-only spans, or streaming placeholders depending on mode.

symbols.does: Compiles an <sc-for> list-rendering directive.
symbols.description: Builds a function that iterates the resolved array, binding each item to the as name and $index, and renders the child builders for each row.

symbols.does: Compiles an <sc-if> conditional directive.
symbols.description: Builds a function that renders children only when the resolved value is truthy, with optional hint placeholder during streaming.

symbols.does: Compiles a <dc-import> component reference.
symbols.description: Collects props, compiles styles, walks children, and returns a builder that creates the host component with __hintSize, __tplId, and __hostStyle metadata.

symbols.does: Compiles an <x-import> external module reference.
symbols.description: Resolves a URL and export/global name, loads the module, and renders the imported component or a placeholder/error if unavailable; rejects bindable URLs.

symbols.does: Evaluates a name getter and strips leftover {{...}} markers.
symbols.description: Helper inside walkXImport that returns the resolved component name string, treating raw braces as empty.

symbols.does: Compiles a plain HTML element into a React element builder.
symbols.description: Resolves raw tag aliases, collects props including style conversion and event mapping, merges pseudo classes, and recursively renders children.

symbols.does: Evaluates the user logic script and returns the Component class.
symbols.description: Uses new Function to run the script in a sandbox with DCLogic, StreamableLogic, and React, returning the defined Component class.

symbols.does: Compares two prop objects ignoring children.
symbols.description: Used by StreamableComponent to decide whether a previously failed constructor can be retried after a registry bump.

symbols.does: Renders a placeholder box for missing or loading components.
symbols.description: React component that shows a sized rectangle, optionally with an error message, used while templates or external modules are unresolved.

symbols.does: Converts a hint-size string into minWidth/minHeight style.
symbols.description: Splits a width,height hint and returns a style object for streaming host sizing.

symbols.does: Creates the component factory and React class used by the runtime.
symbols.description: Builds StreamableComponent, getDC, and Dispatcher, wiring registry subscriptions, logic lifecycle, error boundaries, and ancestor context for cycle detection.

symbols.does: React class that hosts a design component instance.
symbols.description: Manages logic class construction/hot-swap, lifecycle calls, error boundaries, streaming flags, prop overrides, and renders the compiled template inside a sc-host wrapper.

symbols.does: Returns or creates a Dispatcher wrapper for a named component.
symbols.description: Caches a React function component that subscribes to registry updates and renders the corresponding StreamableComponent.

symbols.does: Functional wrapper that subscribes to registry updates for a named DC.
symbols.description: Uses useState/useEffect to re-render when the registry entry bumps, then delegates to StreamableComponent.

symbols.does: Subscription callback inside Dispatcher that increments tick state.
symbols.description: Mirrors the StandaloneRoot subscription pattern, causing the Dispatcher function component to re-render on registry changes.

symbols.does: Checks whether a name looks like a custom element tag.
symbols.description: Returns true if the name contains a hyphen and no dot, used to decide global resolution strategy.

symbols.does: Checks whether a global value is a React component or element.
symbols.description: Distinguishes function components from plain element classes and recognizes React elements by $$typeof.

symbols.does: Walks a dotted path on a root object.
symbols.description: Helper for resolving names like Foo.Bar on window or module exports.

symbols.does: Creates the external module loading and resolution system.
symbols.description: Maintains a cache, loads JS/JSX modules on demand, optionally transpiles JSX with Babel, records new window globals, and reports missing exports.

symbols.does: Loads the Babel standalone compiler from unpkg.
symbols.description: Injects a script tag for @babel/standalone if not already present, returning a promise used before JSX transpilation.

symbols.does: Fetches and evaluates an external module URL.
symbols.description: Resolves the kind, fetches source, transpiles JSX if needed, runs the code in a Function sandbox, captures new window globals, and bumps all components on resolution or failure.

symbols.does: Resolves a named export from a cached external module.
symbols.description: Looks up the named export or default from module.exports or captured globals, logs an error once if missing, and returns the component function.

symbols.does: Polls window or customElements for a global component name.
symbols.description: Used when an x-import references a global name without a URL; polls for up to 30 seconds and bumps the registry when found.

symbols.does: Single polling iteration for waitForGlobal.
symbols.description: Checks for the global component or custom element and schedules the next poll until timeout or success.

symbols.does: Resolves a component from the global scope or a loaded module.
symbols.description: Handles custom elements, dotted global paths, and module globals, returning the renderable value or the name string for unknown custom elements.

symbols.does: Returns the cached error message for an external module/name pair.
symbols.description: Used by walkXImport to display load or missing-export errors in placeholders.

symbols.does: Checks whether a function is an HTMLElement subclass.
symbols.description: Used to exclude plain element classes from being treated as React components.

symbols.does: Creates a manager for injecting <sc-helmet> children into document.head.
symbols.description: Compiles helmet nodes into functions that append scripts, links, meta tags, and live-update title/style-like elements, with streaming-aware partial handling.

symbols.does: Compiles a helmet node into a head-injection function.
symbols.description: Captures child elements and returns a function that idempotently injects scripts/links/meta and updates dynamic tags, skipping possibly-partial last children during streaming.

symbols.does: Creates a dynamic stylesheet for pseudo-class helpers.
symbols.description: Generates unique class names and inserts CSS rules for pseudo-elements/classes requested by style-* attributes.

symbols.does: Creates the component registry.
symbols.description: Provides get/bump helpers over an object map of entries, each holding html, compiled template, Logic class, streaming flags, version, subscribers, and metadata.

symbols.does: Retrieves or creates a registry entry by name.
symbols.description: Returns the existing entry or initializes a default one with empty template/logic and a subscriber Set.

symbols.does: Increments a registry entry version and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Called after any update that should trigger re-renders of subscribed components.

symbols.does: Creates the full runtime instance.
symbols.description: Wires together registry, pseudo sheet, helmet manager, external modules, and component factory, and exposes update/adopt/setProps APIs plus the StreamableLogic base class.

symbols.does: Fetches a sibling .dc.html component by name.
symbols.description: Lazy-loads the component file, parses it, and applies template/logic/props/preview to the registry when not already present.

symbols.does: Updates and compiles a component's template HTML.
symbols.description: Stores the raw HTML, calls compileTemplate, and bumps the registry so subscribers re-render.

symbols.does: Updates and evaluates a component's logic script.
symbols.description: Evaluates the script via evalDcLogic, records logicError if evaluation fails or no Component class is defined, and bumps the registry.

symbols.does: Toggles html or js streaming flags on a registry entry.
symbols.description: Updates the streaming state, toggles the sc-dc-streaming class on the document element if any entry is streaming, and bumps the entry.

symbols.does: Public update entry point for streaming or complete content.
symbols.description: Dispatches html/js/props updates, marks fetched when streaming, and notifies the host after props updates for the root component.

symbols.does: Sets prop overrides on a registry entry.
symbols.description: Stores a clone of the overrides object and bumps the entry so the component re-renders with new props.

symbols.does: Adopts a parsed DC document into the registry.
symbols.description: Used at boot to apply the inline document's template, logic, props, and preview to the root entry.

symbols.does: Hides <x-dc> elements with a CSS rule.
symbols.description: Injects a style tag so raw template markup is not visible before the runtime boots.

symbols.does: Injects a script tag and returns a load promise.
symbols.description: Used to load React UMD scripts with optional integrity; rejects on error.

symbols.does: Loads React and ReactDOM UMD bundles if not present.
symbols.description: Checks window.React/ReactDOM and otherwise loads both scripts in parallel before initialization.

symbols.does: Initializes the runtime and exposes the window API.
symbols.description: Creates the runtime, prepends base CSS, defines __dcUpdate/__dcSetProps/__dcBoot/etc., and boots when the DOM is ready.

symbols.does: Posts the booted state to the parent window.
symbols.description: Sends rootName, propsMeta, and preview via postMessage so an embedding editor knows the runtime is ready.
